meaning: n.四等分,军营或其他住所之分配adj.从斜后侧方向吹来的
examples: He swam quartering upstream, but still was swept downward rather faster than he had expected.
When he had pulled a mile above the village, he started quartering across and bent himself stoutly to his work.
In order to prevent "Chesapeake's biggest shareholders from drawing and quartering" him, McClendon asked Hamm to postpone closing until Aug. 13.
When they made this return journey, they began their characteristic nest-searching behaviour, quartering the ground in detail looking for the entrance, after travelling about ten metres.
